text:
AI is increasingly involved in creative fields, producing art, music, and writing. AI-generated paintings can imitate famous styles, while algorithms compose music that sounds human-made. In writing, AI can draft stories, poems, or news articles quickly.

However, human creativity differs from machine creativity. People create from personal experiences, emotions, and imagination. AI, on the other hand, learns from existing data and follows patterns. It cannot truly feel or invent something entirely new. Instead, it combines and reuses what it has learned.

This raises interesting questions. Can AI ever be truly creative, or is it just a tool that mimics human expression? Many believe that while AI can assist and inspire, genuine creativity remains uniquely human.
